<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://iotdb.apache.org/UserGuide/latest/SQL-Manual/UDF-Libraries_apache.html -->

<!-- page_index: 36 -->

# UDF Libraries

[Skip to main content](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--main-content)

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--udf-libraries"></a>

# UDF Libraries

9/1/23About 82 min

---

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--udf-libraries-2"></a>

# [UDF Libraries](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--udf-libraries)

Based on the ability of user-defined functions, IoTDB provides a series of functions for temporal data processing, including data quality, data profiling, anomaly detection, frequency domain analysis, data matching, data repairing, sequence discovery, machine learning, etc., which can meet the needs of industrial fields for temporal data processing.

> Note: The functions in the current UDF library only support millisecond level timestamp accuracy.

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--_1-installation-steps"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--1.-installation-steps"></a>

## [1. Installation steps](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--_1-installation-steps)

1. Please obtain the compressed file of the UDF library JAR package that is compatible with the IoTDB version.


| UDF installation package | Supported IoTDB versions | Download link |
| --- | --- | --- |
| apache-UDF-1.3.3.zip | V1.3.3 and above | Please contact Timecho for assistance |
| apache-UDF-1.3.2.zip | V1.0.0～V1.3.2 | Please contact Timecho for assistance |

2. Place the library-udf.jar file in the compressed file obtained in the directory `/ext/udf`  of all nodes in the IoTDB cluster
3. In the SQL operation interface of IoTDB's SQL command line terminal (CLI), execute the corresponding function registration statement as follows.
4. Batch registration: Two registration methods: registration script or SQL full statement

- Register Script

  - Copy the registration script ([register-UDF.sh](http://register-UDF.sh) or register-UDF.bat) from the compressed package to the `tools` directory of IoTDB as needed, and modify the parameters in the script (default is host=127.0.0.1, rpcPort=6667, user=root, pass=root);
  - Start IoTDB service, run registration script to batch register UDF
- All SQL statements

  - Open the SQl file in the compressed package, copy all SQL statements, and in the SQL operation interface of IoTDB's SQL command line terminal (CLI), execute all SQl statements to batch register UDFs

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--_2-data-quality"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--2.-data-quality"></a>

## [2. Data Quality](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--_2-data-quality)

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--_2-1-completeness"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--2.1-completeness"></a>

### [2.1 Completeness](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--_2-1-completeness)

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement"></a>

#### [Registration statement](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement)

```sql
create function completeness as 'org.apache.iotdb.library.dquality.UDTFCompleteness'
```

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage"></a>

#### [Usage](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage)

This function calculates the completeness of a time series, which measures the presence or absence of missing values in the time series data. The function divides the input time series data into consecutive non-overlapping time windows, computes the data completeness for each window individually, and outputs the timestamp of the first data point in the window along with the completeness result.

**Name:** COMPLETENESS

**Input Series:** Only support a single input series. The type is INT32 / INT64 / FLOAT / DOUBLE.

**Parameters:**

- `window`: The size of each window. It is a positive integer or a positive number with an unit. The former is the number of data points in each window. The number of data points in the last window may be less than it. The latter is the time of the window. The unit is 'ms' for millisecond, 's' for second, 'm' for minute, 'h' for hour and 'd' for day. By default, all input data belongs to the same window.
- `downtime`: Whether the downtime exception is considered in the calculation of completeness. It is 'true' or 'false' (default). When considering the downtime exception, long-term missing data will be considered as downtime exception without any influence on completeness.

**Output Series:** Output a single series. The type is DOUBLE. The range of each value is [0,1].

**Note:** Only when the number of data points in the window exceeds 10, the calculation will be performed. Otherwise, the window will be ignored and nothing will be output.

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples"></a>

#### [Examples](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples)

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--default-parameters"></a>

##### [Default Parameters](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--default-parameters)

With default parameters, this function will regard all input data as the same window.

Input series:

```
+-----------------------------+---------------+
|                         Time|root.test.d1.s1|
+-----------------------------+---------------+
|2020-01-01T00:00:02.000+08:00|          100.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:03.000+08:00|          101.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:04.000+08:00|          102.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:06.000+08:00|          104.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:08.000+08:00|          126.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:10.000+08:00|          108.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:14.000+08:00|          112.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:15.000+08:00|          113.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:16.000+08:00|          114.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:18.000+08:00|          116.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:20.000+08:00|          118.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:22.000+08:00|          120.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:26.000+08:00|          124.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:28.000+08:00|          126.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:30.000+08:00|            NaN|
+-----------------------------+---------------+
```

SQL for query:

```sql
select completeness(s1) from root.test.d1 where time <= 2020-01-01 00:00:30
```

```
|2020-01-01T00:00:02.000+08:00|                        0.875|
+-----------------------------+-----------------------------+
```

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--_2-2-consistency"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--2.2-consistency"></a>

### [2.2 Consistency](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--_2-2-consistency)

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-1"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-2"></a>

#### [Registration statement](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-1)

```sql
create function consistency as 'org.apache.iotdb.library.dquality.UDTFConsistency'
```

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-1"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-2"></a>

#### [Usage](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-1)

This function calculates the consistency of a time series, which measures whether the changes in the time series data are stable and follow uniform patterns. The function divides the input time series data into consecutive non-overlapping time windows, computes the data consistency for each window individually, and outputs the timestamp of the first data point in the window along with the consistency result.

**Name:** CONSISTENCY

**Input Series:** Only support a single input series. The type is INT32 / INT64 / FLOAT / DOUBLE.

**Parameters:**

- `window`: The size of each window. It is a positive integer or a positive number with an unit. The former is the number of data points in each window. The number of data points in the last window may be less than it. The latter is the time of the window. The unit is 'ms' for millisecond, 's' for second, 'm' for minute, 'h' for hour and 'd' for day. By default, all input data belongs to the same window.

**Output Series:** Output a single series. The type is DOUBLE. The range of each value is [0,1].

**Note:** Only when the number of data points in the window exceeds 10, the calculation will be performed. Otherwise, the window will be ignored and nothing will be output.

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-1"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-2"></a>

#### [Examples](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-1)

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--default-parameters-1"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--default-parameters-2"></a>

##### [Default Parameters](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--default-parameters-1)

With default parameters, this function will regard all input data as the same window.

Input series:

```
+-----------------------------+---------------+
|                         Time|root.test.d1.s1|
+-----------------------------+---------------+
|2020-01-01T00:00:02.000+08:00|          100.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:03.000+08:00|          101.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:04.000+08:00|          102.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:06.000+08:00|          104.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:08.000+08:00|          126.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:10.000+08:00|          108.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:14.000+08:00|          112.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:15.000+08:00|          113.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:16.000+08:00|          114.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:18.000+08:00|          116.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:20.000+08:00|          118.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:22.000+08:00|          120.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:26.000+08:00|          124.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:28.000+08:00|          126.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:30.000+08:00|            NaN|
+-----------------------------+---------------+
```

SQL for query:

```sql
select consistency(s1) from root.test.d1 where time <= 2020-01-01 00:00:30
```

Output series:

```
+-----------------------------+----------------------------+
|                         Time|consistency(root.test.d1.s1)|
+-----------------------------+----------------------------+
|2020-01-01T00:00:02.000+08:00|          0.9333333333333333|
+-----------------------------+----------------------------+
```

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--_2-3-timeliness"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--2.3-timeliness"></a>

### [2.3 Timeliness](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--_2-3-timeliness)

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-2-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-3"></a>

#### [Registration statement](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-2)

```sql
create function timeliness as 'org.apache.iotdb.library.dquality.UDTFTimeliness'
```

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-2-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-3"></a>

#### [Usage](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-2)

This function calculates the timeliness of a time series, which measures whether the time series data is collected and reported on schedule. The function divides the input time series data into consecutive non-overlapping time windows, computes the data timeliness for each window individually, and outputs the timestamp of the first data point in the window along with the timeliness result.

**Name:** TIMELINESS

**Input Series:** Only support a single input series. The type is INT32 / INT64 / FLOAT / DOUBLE.

**Parameters:**

- `window`: The size of each window. It is a positive integer or a positive number with an unit. The former is the number of data points in each window. The number of data points in the last window may be less than it. The latter is the time of the window. The unit is 'ms' for millisecond, 's' for second, 'm' for minute, 'h' for hour and 'd' for day. By default, all input data belongs to the same window.

**Output Series:** Output a single series. The type is DOUBLE. The range of each value is [0,1].

**Note:** Only when the number of data points in the window exceeds 10, the calculation will be performed. Otherwise, the window will be ignored and nothing will be output.

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-2-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-3"></a>

#### [Examples](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-2)

```

```

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--_2-4-validity"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--2.4-validity"></a>

### [2.4 Validity](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--_2-4-validity)

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-3-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-4"></a>

#### [Registration statement](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-3)

```sql
create function validity as 'org.apache.iotdb.library.dquality.UDTFValidity'
```

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-3-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-4"></a>

#### [Usage](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-3)

This function calculates the validity of a time series, which measures whether the time series data is normal, usable, and free of outliers. The function divides the input time series data into consecutive non-overlapping time windows, computes the data validity for each window individually, and outputs the timestamp of the first data point in the window along with the validity result.

**Name:** VALIDITY

**Input Series:** Only support a single input series. The type is INT32 / INT64 / FLOAT / DOUBLE.

**Parameters:**

- `window`: The size of each window. It is a positive integer or a positive number with an unit. The former is the number of data points in each window. The number of data points in the last window may be less than it. The latter is the time of the window. The unit is 'ms' for millisecond, 's' for second, 'm' for minute, 'h' for hour and 'd' for day. By default, all input data belongs to the same window.

**Output Series:** Output a single series. The type is DOUBLE. The range of each value is [0,1].

**Note:** Only when the number of data points in the window exceeds 10, the calculation will be performed. Otherwise, the window will be ignored and nothing will be output.

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-3-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-4"></a>

#### [Examples](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-3)

```

```

```
|2020-01-01T00:00:48.000+08:00|          146.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:50.000+08:00|          148.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:52.000+08:00|          150.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:54.000+08:00|          152.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:56.000+08:00|          154.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:58.000+08:00|          156.0|
|2020-01-01T00:01:00.000+08:00|          158.0|
+-----------------------------+---------------+
```

SQL for query:

```sql
select Validity(s1,"window"="15") from root.test.d1 where time <= 2020-01-01 00:01:00
```

Output series:

```
+-----------------------------+----------------------------------------+
|                         Time|validity(root.test.d1.s1, "window"="15")|
+-----------------------------+----------------------------------------+
|2020-01-01T00:00:02.000+08:00|                      0.8833333333333333|
|2020-01-01T00:00:32.000+08:00|                                     1.0|
+-----------------------------+----------------------------------------+
```

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--_3-data-profiling"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--3.-data-profiling"></a>

## [3. Data Profiling](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--_3-data-profiling)

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--_3-1-acf"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--3.1-acf"></a>

### [3.1 ACF](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--_3-1-acf)

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-4-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-5"></a>

#### [Registration statement](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-4)

```sql
create function acf as 'org.apache.iotdb.library.dprofile.UDTFACF'
```

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-4-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-5"></a>

#### [Usage](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-4)

This function is used to calculate the auto-correlation factor of the input time series, which equals to cross correlation between the same series. For more information, please refer to [XCorr](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--xcorr) function.

**Name:** ACF

**Input Series:** Only support a single input numeric series. The type is INT32 / INT64 / FLOAT / DOUBLE.

**Output Series:** Output a single series. The type is DOUBLE. There are

<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><semantics><mrow><mn>2</mn><mi>N</mi><mo>−</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow><annotation>2N-1</annotation></semantics></math>

2N−1 data points in the series, and the values are interpreted in details in [XCorr](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--xcorr) function.

**Note:**

- `null` and `NaN` values in the input series will be ignored and treated as 0.

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-4-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-5"></a>

#### [Examples](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-4)

Input series:

```
+-----------------------------+---------------+ |                         Time|root.test.d1.s1| +-----------------------------+---------------+ |2020-01-01T00:00:01.000+08:00|              1| |2020-01-01T00:00:02.000+08:00|           null| |2020-01-01T00:00:03.000+08:00|              3| |2020-01-01T00:00:04.000+08:00|            NaN| |2020-01-01T00:00:05.000+08:00|              5| +-----------------------------+---------------+
```

SQL for query:

```sql
select acf(s1) from root.test.d1 where time <= 2020-01-01 00:00:05
```

Output series:

```
+-----------------------------+--------------------+ |                         Time|acf(root.test.d1.s1)| +-----------------------------+--------------------+ |1970-01-01T08:00:00.000+08:00|                 5.0| |1970-01-01T08:00:00.001+08:00|                 0.0| |1970-01-01T08:00:00.002+08:00|                 6.0| |1970-01-01T08:00:00.003+08:00|                 0.0| |1970-01-01T08:00:00.004+08:00|                 7.0| |1970-01-01T08:00:00.005+08:00|                 0.0| |1970-01-01T08:00:00.006+08:00|                 3.6| |1970-01-01T08:00:00.007+08:00|                 0.0| |1970-01-01T08:00:00.008+08:00|                 1.0| +-----------------------------+--------------------+
```

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--_3-2-distinct"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--3.2-distinct"></a>

### [3.2 Distinct](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--_3-2-distinct)

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-5-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-6"></a>

#### [Registration statement](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-5)

```sql
create function distinct as 'org.apache.iotdb.library.dprofile.UDTFDistinct'
```

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-5-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-6"></a>

#### [Usage](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-5)

This function returns all unique values in time series.

**Name:** DISTINCT

**Input Series:** Only support a single input series. The type is arbitrary.

**Output Series:** Output a single series. The type is the same as the input.

**Note:**

- The timestamp of the output series is meaningless. The output order is arbitrary.
- Missing points and null points in the input series will be ignored, but `NaN` will not.
- Case Sensitive.

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-5-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-6"></a>

#### [Examples](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-5)

Input series:

```
+-----------------------------+---------------+ |                         Time|root.test.d2.s2| +-----------------------------+---------------+ |2020-01-01T08:00:00.001+08:00|          Hello| |2020-01-01T08:00:00.002+08:00|          hello| |2020-01-01T08:00:00.003+08:00|          Hello| |2020-01-01T08:00:00.004+08:00|          World| |2020-01-01T08:00:00.005+08:00|          World| +-----------------------------+---------------+
```

SQL for query:

```sql
select distinct(s2) from root.test.d2
```

Output series:

```
+-----------------------------+-------------------------+
|                         Time|distinct(root.test.d2.s2)|
+-----------------------------+-------------------------+
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.001+08:00|                    Hello|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.002+08:00|                    hello|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.003+08:00|                    World|
+-----------------------------+-------------------------+
```

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--_3-3-histogram"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--3.3-histogram"></a>

### [3.3 Histogram](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--_3-3-histogram)

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-6-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-7"></a>

#### [Registration statement](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-6)

```sql
create function histogram as 'org.apache.iotdb.library.dprofile.UDTFHistogram'
```

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-6-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-7"></a>

#### [Usage](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-6)

This function is used to calculate the distribution histogram of a single column of numerical data.

**Name:** HISTOGRAM

**Input Series:** Only supports a single input sequence, the type is INT32 / INT64 / FLOAT / DOUBLE

**Parameters:**

- `min`: The lower limit of the requested data range, the default value is -Double.MAX\_VALUE.
- `max`: The upper limit of the requested data range, the default value is Double.MAX\_VALUE, and the value of start must be less than or equal to end.
- `count`: The number of buckets of the histogram, the default value is 1. It must be a positive integer.

**Output Series:** The value of the bucket of the histogram, where the lower bound represented by the i-th bucket (index starts from 1) is

<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><semantics><mrow><mi>m</mi><mi>i</mi><mi>n</mi><mo>+</mo><mo>(</mo><mi>i</mi><mo>−</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>)</mo><mo>⋅</mo><mfrac><mrow><mi>m</mi><mi>a</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>−</mo><mi>m</mi><mi>i</mi><mi>n</mi></mrow><mrow><mi>c</mi><mi>o</mi><mi>u</mi><mi>n</mi><mi>t</mi></mrow></mfrac></mrow><annotation>min+ (i-1)\cdot\frac{max-min}{count}</annotation></semantics></math>

min+(i−1)⋅countmax−min and the upper bound is

<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><semantics><mrow><mi>m</mi><mi>i</mi><mi>n</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>i</mi><mo>⋅</mo><mfrac><mrow><mi>m</mi><mi>a</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>−</mo><mi>m</mi><mi>i</mi><mi>n</mi></mrow><mrow><mi>c</mi><mi>o</mi><mi>u</mi><mi>n</mi><mi>t</mi></mrow></mfrac></mrow><annotation>min + i \cdot \frac{max-min}{count}</annotation></semantics></math>

min+i⋅countmax−min.

**Note:**

- If the value is lower than `min`, it will be put into the 1st bucket. If the value is larger than `max`, it will be put into the last bucket.
- Missing points, null points and `NaN` in the input series will be ignored.

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-6-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-7"></a>

#### [Examples](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-6)

Input series:

```
+-----------------------------+---------------+
|                         Time|root.test.d1.s1|
+-----------------------------+---------------+
|2020-01-01T00:00:00.000+08:00|            1.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:01.000+08:00|            2.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:02.000+08:00|            3.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:03.000+08:00|            4.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:04.000+08:00|            5.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:05.000+08:00|            6.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:06.000+08:00|            7.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:07.000+08:00|            8.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:08.000+08:00|            9.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:09.000+08:00|           10.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:10.000+08:00|           11.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:11.000+08:00|           12.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:12.000+08:00|           13.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:13.000+08:00|           14.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:14.000+08:00|           15.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:15.000+08:00|           16.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:16.000+08:00|           17.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:17.000+08:00|           18.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:18.000+08:00|           19.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:19.000+08:00|           20.0|
+-----------------------------+---------------+
```

SQL for query:

```sql
select histogram(s1,"min"="1","max"="20","count"="10") from root.test.d1
```

Output series:

```
+-----------------------------+---------------------------------------------------------------+ |                         Time|histogram(root.test.d1.s1, "min"="1", "max"="20", "count"="10")| +-----------------------------+---------------------------------------------------------------+ |1970-01-01T08:00:00.000+08:00|                                                              2| |1970-01-01T08:00:00.001+08:00|                                                              2| |1970-01-01T08:00:00.002+08:00|                                                              2| |1970-01-01T08:00:00.003+08:00|                                                              2| |1970-01-01T08:00:00.004+08:00|                                                              2| |1970-01-01T08:00:00.005+08:00|                                                              2| |1970-01-01T08:00:00.006+08:00|                                                              2| |1970-01-01T08:00:00.007+08:00|                                                              2| |1970-01-01T08:00:00.008+08:00|                                                              2| |1970-01-01T08:00:00.009+08:00|                                                              2| +-----------------------------+---------------------------------------------------------------+
```

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--_3-4-integral"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--3.4-integral"></a>

### [3.4 Integral](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--_3-4-integral)

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-7-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-8"></a>

#### [Registration statement](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-7)

```sql
create function integral as 'org.apache.iotdb.library.dprofile.UDAFIntegral'
```

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-7-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-8"></a>

#### [Usage](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-7)

This function is used to calculate the integration of time series, which equals to the area under the curve with time as X-axis and values as Y-axis.

**Name:** INTEGRAL

**Input Series:** Only support a single input numeric series. The type is INT32 / INT64 / FLOAT / DOUBLE.

**Parameters:**

- `unit`: The unit of time used when computing the integral.
  The value should be chosen from "1S", "1s", "1m", "1H", "1d"(case-sensitive),
  and each represents taking one millisecond / second / minute / hour / day as 1.0 while calculating the area and integral.

**Output Series:** Output a single series. The type is DOUBLE. There is only one data point in the series, whose timestamp is 0 and value is the integration.

**Note:**

- The integral value equals to the sum of the areas of right-angled trapezoids consisting of each two adjacent points and the time-axis.
  Choosing different `unit` implies different scaling of time axis, thus making it apparent to convert the value among those results with constant coefficient.
- `NaN` values in the input series will be ignored. The curve or trapezoids will skip these points and use the next valid point.

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-7-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-8"></a>

#### [Examples](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-7)

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--default-parameters-4-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--default-parameters-5"></a>

##### [Default Parameters](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--default-parameters-4)

With default parameters, this function will take one second as 1.0.

Input series:

```
+-----------------------------+---------------+ |                         Time|root.test.d1.s1| +-----------------------------+---------------+ |2020-01-01T00:00:01.000+08:00|              1| |2020-01-01T00:00:02.000+08:00|              2| |2020-01-01T00:00:03.000+08:00|              5| |2020-01-01T00:00:04.000+08:00|              6| |2020-01-01T00:00:05.000+08:00|              7| |2020-01-01T00:00:08.000+08:00|              8| |2020-01-01T00:00:09.000+08:00|            NaN| |2020-01-01T00:00:10.000+08:00|             10| +-----------------------------+---------------+
```

SQL for query:

```sql
select integral(s1) from root.test.d1 where time <= 2020-01-01 00:00:10
```

Output series:

```
+-----------------------------+-------------------------+
|                         Time|integral(root.test.d1.s1)|
+-----------------------------+-------------------------+
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.000+08:00|                     57.5|
+-----------------------------+-------------------------+
```

Calculation expression:

<math display="block" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><semantics><mrow><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mn>2</mn></mfrac><mo>[</mo><mo>(</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn><mo>)</mo><mo>×</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><mo>(</mo><mn>2</mn><mo>+</mo><mn>5</mn><mo>)</mo><mo>×</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><mo>(</mo><mn>5</mn><mo>+</mo><mn>6</mn><mo>)</mo><mo>×</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><mo>(</mo><mn>6</mn><mo>+</mo><mn>7</mn><mo>)</mo><mo>×</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><mo>(</mo><mn>7</mn><mo>+</mo><mn>8</mn><mo>)</mo><mo>×</mo><mn>3</mn><mo>+</mo><mo>(</mo><mn>8</mn><mo>+</mo><mn>10</mn><mo>)</mo><mo>×</mo><mn>2</mn><mo>]</mo><mo>=</mo><mn>57.5</mn></mrow><annotation>\frac{1}{2}[(1+2) \times 1 + (2+5) \times 1 + (5+6) \times 1 + (6+7) \times 1 + (7+8) \times 3 + (8+10) \times 2] = 57.5 </annotation></semantics></math>

21[(1+2)×1+(2+5)×1+(5+6)×1+(6+7)×1+(7+8)×3+(8+10)×2]=57.5

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--specific-time-unit"></a>

##### [Specific time unit](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--specific-time-unit)

With time unit specified as "1m", this function will take one minute as 1.0.

Input series is the same as above, the SQL for query is shown below:

```sql
select integral(s1, "unit"="1m") from root.test.d1 where time <= 2020-01-01 00:00:10
```

Output series:

```
+-----------------------------+-------------------------+
|                         Time|integral(root.test.d1.s1)|
+-----------------------------+-------------------------+
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.000+08:00|                    0.958|
+-----------------------------+-------------------------+
```

Calculation expression:

<math display="block" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><semantics><mrow><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>×</mo><mn>60</mn></mrow></mfrac><mo>[</mo><mo>(</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn><mo>)</mo><mo>×</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><mo>(</mo><mn>2</mn><mo>+</mo><mn>5</mn><mo>)</mo><mo>×</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><mo>(</mo><mn>5</mn><mo>+</mo><mn>6</mn><mo>)</mo><mo>×</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><mo>(</mo><mn>6</mn><mo>+</mo><mn>7</mn><mo>)</mo><mo>×</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><mo>(</mo><mn>7</mn><mo>+</mo><mn>8</mn><mo>)</mo><mo>×</mo><mn>3</mn><mo>+</mo><mo>(</mo><mn>8</mn><mo>+</mo><mn>10</mn><mo>)</mo><mo>×</mo><mn>2</mn><mo>]</mo><mo>=</mo><mn>0.958</mn></mrow><annotation>\frac{1}{2\times 60}[(1+2) \times 1 + (2+5) \times 1 + (5+6) \times 1 + (6+7) \times 1 + (7+8) \times 3 + (8+10) \times 2] = 0.958 </annotation></semantics></math>

2×601[(1+2)×1+(2+5)×1+(5+6)×1+(6+7)×1+(7+8)×3+(8+10)×2]=0.958

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--_3-5-integralavg"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--3.5-integralavg"></a>

### [3.5 IntegralAvg](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--_3-5-integralavg)

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-8-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-9"></a>

#### [Registration statement](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-8)

```sql
create function integralavg as 'org.apache.iotdb.library.dprofile.UDAFIntegralAvg'
```

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-8-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-9"></a>

#### [Usage](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-8)

This function is used to calculate the function average of time series.
The output equals to the area divided by the time interval using the same time `unit`.
For more information of the area under the curve, please refer to `Integral` function.

**Name:** INTEGRALAVG

**Input Series:** Only support a single input numeric series. The type is INT32 / INT64 / FLOAT / DOUBLE.

**Output Series:** Output a single series. The type is DOUBLE. There is only one data point in the series, whose timestamp is 0 and value is the time-weighted average.

**Note:**

- The time-weighted value equals to the integral value with any `unit` divided by the time interval of input series.
  The result is irrelevant to the time unit used in integral, and it's consistent with the timestamp precision of IoTDB by default.
- `NaN` values in the input series will be ignored. The curve or trapezoids will skip these points and use the next valid point.
- If the input series is empty, the output value will be 0.0, but if there is only one data point, the value will equal to the input value.

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-8-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-9"></a>

#### [Examples](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-8)

Input series:

```
+-----------------------------+---------------+ |                         Time|root.test.d1.s1| +-----------------------------+---------------+ |2020-01-01T00:00:01.000+08:00|              1| |2020-01-01T00:00:02.000+08:00|              2| |2020-01-01T00:00:03.000+08:00|              5| |2020-01-01T00:00:04.000+08:00|              6| |2020-01-01T00:00:05.000+08:00|              7| |2020-01-01T00:00:08.000+08:00|              8| |2020-01-01T00:00:09.000+08:00|            NaN| |2020-01-01T00:00:10.000+08:00|             10| +-----------------------------+---------------+
```

SQL for query:

```sql
select integralavg(s1) from root.test.d1 where time <= 2020-01-01 00:00:10
```

Output series:

```
+-----------------------------+----------------------------+
|                         Time|integralavg(root.test.d1.s1)|
+-----------------------------+----------------------------+
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.000+08:00|           6.388888888888889|
+-----------------------------+----------------------------+
```

Calculation expression:

<math display="block" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><semantics><mrow><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mn>2</mn></mfrac><mo>[</mo><mo>(</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn><mo>)</mo><mo>×</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><mo>(</mo><mn>2</mn><mo>+</mo><mn>5</mn><mo>)</mo><mo>×</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><mo>(</mo><mn>5</mn><mo>+</mo><mn>6</mn><mo>)</mo><mo>×</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><mo>(</mo><mn>6</mn><mo>+</mo><mn>7</mn><mo>)</mo><mo>×</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><mo>(</mo><mn>7</mn><mo>+</mo><mn>8</mn><mo>)</mo><mo>×</mo><mn>3</mn><mo>+</mo><mo>(</mo><mn>8</mn><mo>+</mo><mn>10</mn><mo>)</mo><mo>×</mo><mn>2</mn><mo>]</mo><mi>/</mi><mn>10</mn><mo>=</mo><mn>5.75</mn></mrow><annotation>\frac{1}{2}[(1+2) \times 1 + (2+5) \times 1 + (5+6) \times 1 + (6+7) \times 1 + (7+8) \times 3 + (8+10) \times 2] / 10 = 5.75 </annotation></semantics></math>

21[(1+2)×1+(2+5)×1+(5+6)×1+(6+7)×1+(7+8)×3+(8+10)×2]/10=5.75

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--_3-6-mad"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--3.6-mad"></a>

### [3.6 Mad](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--_3-6-mad)

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-9-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-10"></a>

#### [Registration statement](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-9)

```sql
create function mad as 'org.apache.iotdb.library.dprofile.UDAFMad'
```

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-9-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-10"></a>

#### [Usage](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-9)

The function is used to compute the exact or approximate median absolute deviation (MAD) of a numeric time series. MAD is the median of the deviation of each element from the elements' median.

Take a dataset

<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><semantics><mrow><mo>{</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>3</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>3</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>5</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>5</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>6</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>7</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>8</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>9</mn><mo>}</mo></mrow><annotation>\{1,3,3,5,5,6,7,8,9\}</annotation></semantics></math>

{1,3,3,5,5,6,7,8,9} as an instance. Its median is 5 and the deviation of each element from the median is

<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><semantics><mrow><mo>{</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>2</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>2</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>2</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>3</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>4</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>4</mn><mo>}</mo></mrow><annotation>\{0,0,1,2,2,2,3,4,4\}</annotation></semantics></math>

{0,0,1,2,2,2,3,4,4}, whose median is 2. Therefore, the MAD of the original dataset is 2.

**Name:** MAD

**Input Series:** Only support a single input series. The data type is INT32 / INT64 / FLOAT / DOUBLE.

**Parameter:**

- `error`: The relative error of the approximate MAD. It should be within [0,1) and the default value is 0. Taking `error`=0.01 as an instance, suppose the exact MAD is

<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><semantics><mrow><mi>a</mi></mrow><annotation>a</annotation></semantics></math>

  a and the approximate MAD is

<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><semantics><mrow><mi>b</mi></mrow><annotation>b</annotation></semantics></math>

  b, we have

<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><semantics><mrow><mn>0.99</mn><mi>a</mi><mo>≤</mo><mi>b</mi><mo>≤</mo><mn>1.01</mn><mi>a</mi></mrow><annotation>0.99a \le b \le 1.01a</annotation></semantics></math>

  0.99a≤b≤1.01a. With `error`=0, the output is the exact MAD.

**Output Series:** Output a single series. The type is DOUBLE. There is only one data point in the series, whose timestamp is 0 and value is the MAD.

**Note:** Missing points, null points and `NaN` in the input series will be ignored.

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-9-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-10"></a>

#### [Examples](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-9)

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--approximate-query"></a>

##### [Approximate Query](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--approximate-query)

By setting `error` within (0,1), the function queries the approximate MAD.

Input series:

```
+-----------------------------+------------+
|                         Time|root.test.s1|
+-----------------------------+------------+
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.100+08:00|         0.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.200+08:00|         0.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.300+08:00|         1.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.400+08:00|        -1.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.500+08:00|         0.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.600+08:00|         0.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.700+08:00|        -2.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.800+08:00|         2.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.900+08:00|         0.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.000+08:00|         0.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.100+08:00|         1.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.200+08:00|        -1.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.300+08:00|        -1.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.400+08:00|         1.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.500+08:00|         0.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.600+08:00|         0.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.700+08:00|        10.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.800+08:00|         2.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.900+08:00|        -2.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:02.000+08:00|         0.0|
+-----------------------------+------------+
............
Total line number = 20
```

SQL for query:

```sql
select mad(s1, "error"="0.01") from root.test
```

Output series:

```
+-----------------------------+---------------------------------+
|                         Time|mad(root.test.s1, "error"="0.01")|
+-----------------------------+---------------------------------+
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.000+08:00|               0.9900000000000001|
+-----------------------------+---------------------------------+
```

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--_3-7-median"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--3.7-median"></a>

### [3.7 Median](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--_3-7-median)

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-10-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-11"></a>

#### [Registration statement](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-10)

```sql
create function median as 'org.apache.iotdb.library.dprofile.UDAFMedian'
```

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-10-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-11"></a>

#### [Usage](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-10)

The function is used to compute the exact or approximate median of a numeric time series. Median is the value separating the higher half from the lower half of a data sample.

**Name:** MEDIAN

**Input Series:** Only support a single input series. The data type is INT32 / INT64 / FLOAT / DOUBLE.

**Parameter:**

- `error`: The rank error of the approximate median. It should be within [0,1) and the default value is 0. For instance, a median with `error`=0.01 is the value of the element with rank percentage 0.49~0.51. With `error`=0, the output is the exact median.

**Output Series:** Output a single series. The type is DOUBLE. There is only one data point in the series, whose timestamp is 0 and value is the median.

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-10-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-11"></a>

#### [Examples](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-10)

Input series:

```
+-----------------------------+------------+
|                         Time|root.test.s1|
+-----------------------------+------------+
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.100+08:00|         0.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.200+08:00|         0.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.300+08:00|         1.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.400+08:00|        -1.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.500+08:00|         0.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.600+08:00|         0.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.700+08:00|        -2.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.800+08:00|         2.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.900+08:00|         0.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.000+08:00|         0.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.100+08:00|         1.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.200+08:00|        -1.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.300+08:00|        -1.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.400+08:00|         1.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.500+08:00|         0.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.600+08:00|         0.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.700+08:00|        10.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.800+08:00|         2.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.900+08:00|        -2.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:02.000+08:00|         0.0|
+-----------------------------+------------+
Total line number = 20
```

SQL for query:

```sql
select median(s1, "error"="0.01") from root.test
```

Output series:

```
+-----------------------------+------------------------------------+
|                         Time|median(root.test.s1, "error"="0.01")|
+-----------------------------+------------------------------------+
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.000+08:00|                                 0.0|
+-----------------------------+------------------------------------+
```

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--_3-8-minmax"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--3.8-minmax"></a>

### [3.8 MinMax](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--_3-8-minmax)

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-11-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-12"></a>

#### [Registration statement](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-11)

```sql
create function minmax as 'org.apache.iotdb.library.dprofile.UDTFMinMax'
```

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-11-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-12"></a>

#### [Usage](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-11)

This function is used to standardize the input series with min-max. Minimum value is transformed to 0; maximum value is transformed to 1.

**Name:** MINMAX

**Input Series:** Only support a single input series. The type is INT32 / INT64 / FLOAT / DOUBLE.

- `compute`: When set to "batch", anomaly test is conducted after importing all data points; when set to "stream", it is required to provide minimum and maximum values. The default method is "batch".
- `min`: The maximum value when method is set to "stream".
- `max`: The minimum value when method is set to "stream".

**Output Series:** Output a single series. The type is DOUBLE.

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-11-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-12"></a>

#### [Examples](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-11)

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--batch-computing"></a>

##### [Batch computing](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--batch-computing)

Input series:

```
+-----------------------------+------------+
|                         Time|root.test.s1|
+-----------------------------+------------+
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.100+08:00|         0.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.200+08:00|         0.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.300+08:00|         1.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.400+08:00|        -1.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.500+08:00|         0.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.600+08:00|         0.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.700+08:00|        -2.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.800+08:00|         2.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.900+08:00|         0.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.000+08:00|         0.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.100+08:00|         1.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.200+08:00|        -1.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.300+08:00|        -1.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.400+08:00|         1.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.500+08:00|         0.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.600+08:00|         0.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.700+08:00|        10.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.800+08:00|         2.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.900+08:00|        -2.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:02.000+08:00|         0.0|
+-----------------------------+------------+
```

SQL for query:

```sql
select minmax(s1) from root.test
```

Output series:

```
+-----------------------------+--------------------+
|                         Time|minmax(root.test.s1)|
+-----------------------------+--------------------+
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.100+08:00| 0.16666666666666666|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.200+08:00| 0.16666666666666666|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.300+08:00|                0.25|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.400+08:00| 0.08333333333333333|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.500+08:00| 0.16666666666666666|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.600+08:00| 0.16666666666666666|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.700+08:00|                 0.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.800+08:00|  0.3333333333333333|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.900+08:00| 0.16666666666666666|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.000+08:00| 0.16666666666666666|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.100+08:00|                0.25|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.200+08:00| 0.08333333333333333|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.300+08:00| 0.08333333333333333|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.400+08:00|                0.25|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.500+08:00| 0.16666666666666666|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.600+08:00| 0.16666666666666666|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.700+08:00|                 1.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.800+08:00|  0.3333333333333333|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.900+08:00|                 0.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:02.000+08:00| 0.16666666666666666|
```

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--_3-9-mvavg"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--3.9-mvavg"></a>

### [3.9 MvAvg](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--_3-9-mvavg)

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-12-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-13"></a>

#### [Registration statement](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-12)

```sql
create function mvavg as 'org.apache.iotdb.library.dprofile.UDTFMvAvg'
```

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-12-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-13"></a>

#### [Usage](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-12)

This function is used to calculate moving average of input series.

**Name:** MVAVG

**Input Series:** Only support a single input series. The type is INT32 / INT64 / FLOAT / DOUBLE.

- `window`: Length of the moving window. Default value is 10.

**Output Series:** Output a single series. The type is DOUBLE.

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-12-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-13"></a>

#### [Examples](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-12)

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--batch-computing-1"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--batch-computing-2"></a>

##### [Batch computing](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--batch-computing-1)

Input series:

```
+-----------------------------+------------+
|                         Time|root.test.s1|
+-----------------------------+------------+
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.100+08:00|         0.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.200+08:00|         0.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.300+08:00|         1.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.400+08:00|        -1.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.500+08:00|         0.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.600+08:00|         0.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.700+08:00|        -2.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.800+08:00|         2.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.900+08:00|         0.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.000+08:00|         0.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.100+08:00|         1.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.200+08:00|        -1.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.300+08:00|        -1.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.400+08:00|         1.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.500+08:00|         0.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.600+08:00|         0.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.700+08:00|        10.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.800+08:00|         2.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.900+08:00|        -2.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:02.000+08:00|         0.0|
+-----------------------------+------------+
```

SQL for query:

```sql
select mvavg(s1, "window"="3") from root.test
```

Output series:

```
+-----------------------------+---------------------------------+
|                         Time|mvavg(root.test.s1, "window"="3")|
+-----------------------------+---------------------------------+
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.300+08:00|               0.3333333333333333|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.400+08:00|              -0.3333333333333333|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.500+08:00|                              0.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.600+08:00|              -0.3333333333333333|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.700+08:00|              -0.3333333333333333|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.800+08:00|               0.6666666666666666|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.900+08:00|                              0.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.000+08:00|               0.6666666666666666|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.100+08:00|              -0.3333333333333333|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.200+08:00|              -0.3333333333333333|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.300+08:00|              -0.3333333333333333|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.400+08:00|                              0.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.500+08:00|               0.3333333333333333|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.600+08:00|               0.3333333333333333|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.700+08:00|                              3.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.800+08:00|               0.6666666666666666|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.900+08:00|              -0.6666666666666666|
|1970-01-01T08:00:02.000+08:00|              -3.3333333333333335|
+-----------------------------+---------------------------------+
```

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--_3-10-pacf"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--3.10-pacf"></a>

### [3.10 PACF](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--_3-10-pacf)

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-13-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-14"></a>

#### [Registration statement](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-13)

```sql
create function pacf as 'org.apache.iotdb.library.dprofile.UDTFPACF'
```

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-13-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-14"></a>

#### [Usage](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-13)

This function is used to calculate partial autocorrelation of input series by solving Yule-Walker equation. For some cases, the equation may not be solved, and NaN will be output.

**Name:** PACF

**Input Series:** Only support a single input series. The type is INT32 / INT64 / FLOAT / DOUBLE.

- `lag`: Maximum lag of pacf to calculate. The default value is

<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><semantics><mrow><mi>min</mi><mo>⁡</mo><mo>(</mo><mn>10</mn><msub><mrow><mi>log</mi><mo>⁡</mo></mrow><mn>10</mn></msub><mi>n</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>n</mi><mo>−</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>)</mo></mrow><annotation>\min(10\log_{10}n,n-1)</annotation></semantics></math>

  min(10log10n,n−1), where

<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><semantics><mrow><mi>n</mi></mrow><annotation>n</annotation></semantics></math>

  n is the number of data points.

**Output Series:** Output a single series. The type is DOUBLE.

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-13-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-14"></a>

#### [Examples](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-13)

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--assigning-maximum-lag"></a>

##### [Assigning maximum lag](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--assigning-maximum-lag)

Input series:

```
+-----------------------------+---------------+ |                         Time|root.test.d1.s1| +-----------------------------+---------------+ |2020-01-01T00:00:01.000+08:00|              1| |2020-01-01T00:00:02.000+08:00|            NaN| |2020-01-01T00:00:03.000+08:00|              3| |2020-01-01T00:00:04.000+08:00|            NaN| |2020-01-01T00:00:05.000+08:00|              5| +-----------------------------+---------------+
```

SQL for query:

```sql
select pacf(s1, "lag"="5") from root.test.d1
```

Output series:

```
+-----------------------------+--------------------------------+ |                         Time|pacf(root.test.d1.s1, "lag"="5")| +-----------------------------+--------------------------------+ |2020-01-01T00:00:01.000+08:00|                             1.0| |2020-01-01T00:00:02.000+08:00|             -0.5744680851063829| |2020-01-01T00:00:03.000+08:00|              0.3172297297297296| |2020-01-01T00:00:04.000+08:00|             -0.2977686586304181| |2020-01-01T00:00:05.000+08:00|             -2.0609033521065867| +-----------------------------+--------------------------------+
```

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--_3-11-percentile"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--3.11-percentile"></a>

### [3.11 Percentile](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--_3-11-percentile)

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-14-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-15"></a>

#### [Registration statement](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-14)

```sql
create function percentile as 'org.apache.iotdb.library.dprofile.UDAFPercentile'
```

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-14-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-15"></a>

#### [Usage](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-14)

The function is used to compute the exact or approximate percentile of a numeric time series. A percentile is value of element in the certain rank of the sorted series.

**Name:** PERCENTILE

**Input Series:** Only support a single input series. The data type is INT32 / INT64 / FLOAT / DOUBLE.

**Parameter:**

- `rank`: The rank percentage of the percentile. It should be (0,1] and the default value is 0.5. For instance, a percentile with `rank`=0.5 is the median.
- `error`: The rank error of the approximate percentile. It should be within [0,1) and the default value is 0. For instance, a 0.5-percentile with `error`=0.01 is the value of the element with rank percentage 0.49~0.51. With `error`=0, the output is the exact percentile.

**Output Series:** Output a single series. The type is the same as input series. If `error`=0, there is only one data point in the series, whose timestamp is the same has which the first percentile value has, and value is the percentile, otherwise the timestamp of the only data point is 0.

**Note:** Missing points, null points and `NaN` in the input series will be ignored.

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-14-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-15"></a>

#### [Examples](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-14)

Input series:

```
+-----------------------------+-------------+
|                         Time|root.test2.s1|
+-----------------------------+-------------+
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.100+08:00|          0.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.200+08:00|          0.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.300+08:00|          1.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.400+08:00|         -1.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.500+08:00|          0.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.600+08:00|          0.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.700+08:00|         -2.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.800+08:00|          2.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.900+08:00|          0.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.000+08:00|          0.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.100+08:00|          1.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.200+08:00|         -1.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.300+08:00|         -1.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.400+08:00|          1.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.500+08:00|          0.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.600+08:00|          0.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.700+08:00|         10.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.800+08:00|          2.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.900+08:00|         -2.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:02.000+08:00|          0.0|
+-----------------------------+-------------+
Total line number = 20
```

SQL for query:

```sql
select percentile(s0, "rank"="0.2", "error"="0.01") from root.test
```

Output series:

```
+-----------------------------+-------------------------------------------------------+
|                         Time|percentile(root.test2.s1, "rank"="0.2", "error"="0.01")|
+-----------------------------+-------------------------------------------------------+
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.000+08:00|                                                   -1.0|
+-----------------------------+-------------------------------------------------------+
```

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--_3-12-quantile"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--3.12-quantile"></a>

### [3.12 Quantile](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--_3-12-quantile)

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-15-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-16"></a>

#### [Registration statement](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-15)

```sql
create function quantile as 'org.apache.iotdb.library.dprofile.UDAFQuantile'
```

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-15-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-16"></a>

#### [Usage](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-15)

The function is used to compute the approximate quantile of a numeric time series. A quantile is value of element in the certain rank of the sorted series.

**Name:** QUANTILE

**Input Series:** Only support a single input series. The data type is INT32 / INT64 / FLOAT / DOUBLE.

**Parameter:**

- `rank`: The rank of the quantile. It should be (0,1] and the default value is 0.5. For instance, a quantile with `rank`=0.5 is the median.
- `K`: The size of KLL sketch maintained in the query. It should be within [100,+inf) and the default value is 800. For instance, the 0.5-quantile computed by a KLL sketch with K=800 items is a value with rank quantile 0.49~0.51 with a confidence of at least 99%. The result will be more accurate as K increases.

**Output Series:** Output a single series. The type is the same as input series. The timestamp of the only data point is 0.

**Note:** Missing points, null points and `NaN` in the input series will be ignored.

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-15-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-16"></a>

#### [Examples](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-15)

Input series:

```
+-----------------------------+-------------+ |                         Time|root.test1.s1| +-----------------------------+-------------+ |2021-03-17T10:32:17.054+08:00|            7| |2021-03-17T10:32:18.054+08:00|           15| |2021-03-17T10:32:19.054+08:00|           36| |2021-03-17T10:32:20.054+08:00|           39| |2021-03-17T10:32:21.054+08:00|           40| |2021-03-17T10:32:22.054+08:00|           41| |2021-03-17T10:32:23.054+08:00|           20| |2021-03-17T10:32:24.054+08:00|           18| +-----------------------------+-------------+ ............Total line number = 8
```

SQL for query:

```sql
select quantile(s1, "rank"="0.2", "K"="800") from root.test1
```

Output series:

```
+-----------------------------+------------------------------------------------+
|                         Time|quantile(root.test1.s1, "rank"="0.2", "K"="800")|
+-----------------------------+------------------------------------------------+
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.000+08:00|                               7.000000000000001|
+-----------------------------+------------------------------------------------+
```

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--_3-13-period"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--3.13-period"></a>

### [3.13 Period](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--_3-13-period)

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-16-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-17"></a>

#### [Registration statement](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-16)

```sql
create function period as 'org.apache.iotdb.library.dprofile.UDAFPeriod'
```

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-16-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-17"></a>

#### [Usage](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-16)

The function is used to compute the period of a numeric time series.

**Name:** PERIOD

**Input Series:** Only support a single input series. The data type is INT32 / INT64 / FLOAT / DOUBLE.

**Output Series:** Output a single series. The type is INT32. There is only one data point in the series, whose timestamp is 0 and value is the period.

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-16-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-17"></a>

#### [Examples](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-16)

Input series:

```
+-----------------------------+---------------+ |                         Time|root.test.d3.s1| +-----------------------------+---------------+ |1970-01-01T08:00:00.001+08:00|            1.0| |1970-01-01T08:00:00.002+08:00|            2.0| |1970-01-01T08:00:00.003+08:00|            3.0| |1970-01-01T08:00:00.004+08:00|            1.0| |1970-01-01T08:00:00.005+08:00|            2.0| |1970-01-01T08:00:00.006+08:00|            3.0| |1970-01-01T08:00:00.007+08:00|            1.0| |1970-01-01T08:00:00.008+08:00|            2.0| |1970-01-01T08:00:00.009+08:00|            3.0| +-----------------------------+---------------+
```

SQL for query:

```sql
select period(s1) from root.test.d3
```

Output series:

```
+-----------------------------+-----------------------+
|                         Time|period(root.test.d3.s1)|
+-----------------------------+-----------------------+
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.000+08:00|                      3|
+-----------------------------+-----------------------+
```

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--_3-14-qlb"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--3.14-qlb"></a>

### [3.14 QLB](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--_3-14-qlb)

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-17-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-18"></a>

#### [Registration statement](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-17)

```sql
create function qlb as 'org.apache.iotdb.library.dprofile.UDTFQLB'
```

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-17-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-18"></a>

#### [Usage](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-17)

This function is used to calculate Ljung-Box statistics

<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><semantics><mrow><msub><mi>Q</mi><mrow><mi>L</mi><mi>B</mi></mrow></msub></mrow><annotation>Q_{LB}</annotation></semantics></math>

QLB for time series, and convert it to p value.

**Name:** QLB

**Input Series:** Only support a single input series. The type is INT32 / INT64 / FLOAT / DOUBLE.

**Parameters**:

`lag`: max lag to calculate. Legal input shall be integer from 1 to n-2, where n is the sample number. Default value is n-2.

**Output Series:** Output a single series. The type is DOUBLE. The output series is p value, and timestamp means lag.

**Note:** If you want to calculate Ljung-Box statistics

<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><semantics><mrow><msub><mi>Q</mi><mrow><mi>L</mi><mi>B</mi></mrow></msub></mrow><annotation>Q_{LB}</annotation></semantics></math>

QLB instead of p value, you may use ACF function.

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-17-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-18"></a>

#### [Examples](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-17)

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--using-default-parameter"></a>

##### [Using Default Parameter](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--using-default-parameter)

Input series:

```
+-----------------------------+---------------+
|                         Time|root.test.d1.s1|
+-----------------------------+---------------+
|1970-01-01T00:00:00.100+08:00|           1.22|
|1970-01-01T00:00:00.200+08:00|          -2.78|
|1970-01-01T00:00:00.300+08:00|           1.53|
|1970-01-01T00:00:00.400+08:00|           0.70|
|1970-01-01T00:00:00.500+08:00|           0.75|
|1970-01-01T00:00:00.600+08:00|          -0.72|
|1970-01-01T00:00:00.700+08:00|          -0.22|
|1970-01-01T00:00:00.800+08:00|           0.28|
|1970-01-01T00:00:00.900+08:00|           0.57|
|1970-01-01T00:00:01.000+08:00|          -0.22|
|1970-01-01T00:00:01.100+08:00|          -0.72|
|1970-01-01T00:00:01.200+08:00|           1.34|
|1970-01-01T00:00:01.300+08:00|          -0.25|
|1970-01-01T00:00:01.400+08:00|           0.17|
|1970-01-01T00:00:01.500+08:00|           2.51|
|1970-01-01T00:00:01.600+08:00|           1.42|
|1970-01-01T00:00:01.700+08:00|          -1.34|
|1970-01-01T00:00:01.800+08:00|          -0.01|
|1970-01-01T00:00:01.900+08:00|          -0.49|
|1970-01-01T00:00:02.000+08:00|           1.63|
+-----------------------------+---------------+
```

```sql
select QLB(s1) from root.test.d1
```

```
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.031+08:00|  0.35853999999999997|
```
