<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--_3-15-resample"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--3.15-resample"></a>

### [3.15 Resample](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--_3-15-resample)

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-18-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-19"></a>

#### [Registration statement](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-18)

```sql
create function re_sample as 'org.apache.iotdb.library.dprofile.UDTFResample'
```

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-18-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-19"></a>

#### [Usage](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-18)

This function is used to resample the input series according to a given frequency, including up-sampling and down-sampling.
Currently, the supported up-sampling methods are
NaN (filling with `NaN`), FFill (filling with previous value), BFill (filling with next value) and
Linear (filling with linear interpolation).
Down-sampling relies on group aggregation, which supports Max, Min, First, Last, Mean and Median.

**Name:** RESAMPLE

**Input Series:** Only support a single input series. The type is INT32 / INT64 / FLOAT / DOUBLE.

**Parameters:**

- `every`: The frequency of resampling, which is a positive number with an unit. The unit is 'ms' for millisecond, 's' for second, 'm' for minute, 'h' for hour and 'd' for day. This parameter cannot be lacked.
- `interp`: The interpolation method of up-sampling, which is 'NaN', 'FFill', 'BFill' or 'Linear'. By default, NaN is used.
- `aggr`: The aggregation method of down-sampling, which is 'Max', 'Min', 'First', 'Last', 'Mean' or 'Median'. By default, Mean is used.
- `start`: The start time (inclusive) of resampling with the format 'yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss'. By default, it is the timestamp of the first valid data point.
- `end`: The end time (exclusive) of resampling with the format 'yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss'. By default, it is the timestamp of the last valid data point.

**Output Series:** Output a single series. The type is DOUBLE. It is strictly equispaced with the frequency `every`.

**Note:** `NaN` in the input series will be ignored.

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-18-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-19"></a>

#### [Examples](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-18)

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--up-sampling"></a>

##### [Up-sampling](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--up-sampling)

When the frequency of resampling is higher than the original frequency, up-sampling starts.

Input series:

```
+-----------------------------+---------------+ |                         Time|root.test.d1.s1| +-----------------------------+---------------+ |2021-03-06T16:00:00.000+08:00|           3.09| |2021-03-06T16:15:00.000+08:00|           3.53| |2021-03-06T16:30:00.000+08:00|            3.5| |2021-03-06T16:45:00.000+08:00|           3.51| |2021-03-06T17:00:00.000+08:00|           3.41| +-----------------------------+---------------+
```

SQL for query:

```sql
select resample(s1,'every'='5m','interp'='linear') from root.test.d1
```

Output series:

```
+-----------------------------+----------------------------------------------------------+
|                         Time|resample(root.test.d1.s1, "every"="5m", "interp"="linear")|
+-----------------------------+----------------------------------------------------------+
|2021-03-06T16:00:00.000+08:00|                                        3.0899999141693115|
|2021-03-06T16:05:00.000+08:00|                                        3.2366665999094644|
|2021-03-06T16:10:00.000+08:00|                                        3.3833332856496177|
|2021-03-06T16:15:00.000+08:00|                                        3.5299999713897705|
|2021-03-06T16:20:00.000+08:00|                                        3.5199999809265137|
|2021-03-06T16:25:00.000+08:00|                                         3.509999990463257|
|2021-03-06T16:30:00.000+08:00|                                                       3.5|
|2021-03-06T16:35:00.000+08:00|                                         3.503333330154419|
|2021-03-06T16:40:00.000+08:00|                                         3.506666660308838|
|2021-03-06T16:45:00.000+08:00|                                         3.509999990463257|
|2021-03-06T16:50:00.000+08:00|                                        3.4766666889190674|
|2021-03-06T16:55:00.000+08:00|                                         3.443333387374878|
|2021-03-06T17:00:00.000+08:00|                                        3.4100000858306885|
+-----------------------------+----------------------------------------------------------+
```

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--down-sampling"></a>

##### [Down-sampling](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--down-sampling)

When the frequency of resampling is lower than the original frequency, down-sampling starts.

Input series is the same as above, the SQL for query is shown below:

```sql
select resample(s1,'every'='30m','aggr'='first') from root.test.d1
```

Output series:

```
+-----------------------------+--------------------------------------------------------+
|                         Time|resample(root.test.d1.s1, "every"="30m", "aggr"="first")|
+-----------------------------+--------------------------------------------------------+
|2021-03-06T16:00:00.000+08:00|                                      3.0899999141693115|
|2021-03-06T16:30:00.000+08:00|                                                     3.5|
|2021-03-06T17:00:00.000+08:00|                                      3.4100000858306885|
+-----------------------------+--------------------------------------------------------+
```

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--specify-the-time-period"></a>

##### [Specify the time period](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--specify-the-time-period)

The time period of resampling can be specified with `start` and `end`.
The period outside the actual time range will be interpolated.

Input series is the same as above, the SQL for query is shown below:

```sql
select resample(s1,'every'='30m','start'='2021-03-06 15:00:00') from root.test.d1
```

Output series:

```
+-----------------------------+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+ |                         Time|resample(root.test.d1.s1, "every"="30m", "start"="2021-03-06 15:00:00")| +-----------------------------+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+ |2021-03-06T15:00:00.000+08:00|                                                                    NaN| |2021-03-06T15:30:00.000+08:00|                                                                    NaN| |2021-03-06T16:00:00.000+08:00|                                                      3.309999942779541| |2021-03-06T16:30:00.000+08:00|                                                     3.5049999952316284| |2021-03-06T17:00:00.000+08:00|                                                     3.4100000858306885| +-----------------------------+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
```

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--_3-16-sample"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--3.16-sample"></a>

### [3.16 Sample](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--_3-16-sample)

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-19-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-20"></a>

#### [Registration statement](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-19)

```sql
create function sample as 'org.apache.iotdb.library.dprofile.UDTFSample'
```

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-19-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-20"></a>

#### [Usage](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-19)

This function is used to sample the input series, that is, select a specified number of data points from the input series and output them. Currently, three sampling methods are supported: **Reservoir sampling** randomly selects data points. All of the points have the same probability of being sampled. **Isometric sampling** selects data points at equal index intervals. **Triangle sampling** assigns data points to the buckets based on the number of sampling. Then it calculates the area of the triangle based on these points inside the bucket and selects the point with the largest area of the triangle. For more detail, please read [paper](http://skemman.is/stream/get/1946/15343/37285/3/SS_MSthesis.pdf)

**Name:** SAMPLE

**Input Series:** Only support a single input series. The type is arbitrary.

**Parameters:**

- `method`: The method of sampling, which is 'reservoir', 'isometric' or 'triangle'. By default, reservoir sampling is used.
- `k`: The number of sampling, which is a positive integer. By default, it's 1.

**Output Series:** Output a single series. The type is the same as the input. The length of the output series is `k`. Each data point in the output series comes from the input series.

**Note:** If `k` is greater than the length of input series, all data points in the input series will be output.

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-19-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-20"></a>

#### [Examples](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-19)

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--reservoir-sampling"></a>

##### [Reservoir Sampling](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--reservoir-sampling)

When `method` is 'reservoir' or the default, reservoir sampling is used.
Due to the randomness of this method, the output series shown below is only a possible result.

Input series:

```
+-----------------------------+---------------+ |                         Time|root.test.d1.s1| +-----------------------------+---------------+ |2020-01-01T00:00:01.000+08:00|            1.0| |2020-01-01T00:00:02.000+08:00|            2.0| |2020-01-01T00:00:03.000+08:00|            3.0| |2020-01-01T00:00:04.000+08:00|            4.0| |2020-01-01T00:00:05.000+08:00|            5.0| |2020-01-01T00:00:06.000+08:00|            6.0| |2020-01-01T00:00:07.000+08:00|            7.0| |2020-01-01T00:00:08.000+08:00|            8.0| |2020-01-01T00:00:09.000+08:00|            9.0| |2020-01-01T00:00:10.000+08:00|           10.0| +-----------------------------+---------------+
```

SQL for query:

```sql
select sample(s1,'method'='reservoir','k'='5') from root.test.d1
```

Output series:

```
+-----------------------------+------------------------------------------------------+ |                         Time|sample(root.test.d1.s1, "method"="reservoir", "k"="5")| +-----------------------------+------------------------------------------------------+ |2020-01-01T00:00:01.000+08:00|                                                   1.0| |2020-01-01T00:00:05.000+08:00|                                                   5.0| |2020-01-01T00:00:06.000+08:00|                                                   6.0| |2020-01-01T00:00:08.000+08:00|                                                   8.0| |2020-01-01T00:00:09.000+08:00|                                                   9.0| +-----------------------------+------------------------------------------------------+
```

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--isometric-sampling"></a>

##### [Isometric Sampling](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--isometric-sampling)

When `method` is 'isometric', isometric sampling is used.

Input series is the same as above, the SQL for query is shown below:

```sql
select sample(s1,'method'='isometric','k'='5') from root.test.d1
```

Output series:

```
+-----------------------------+------------------------------------------------------+ |                         Time|sample(root.test.d1.s1, "method"="isometric", "k"="5")| +-----------------------------+------------------------------------------------------+ |2020-01-01T00:00:01.000+08:00|                                                   1.0| |2020-01-01T00:00:03.000+08:00|                                                   3.0| |2020-01-01T00:00:05.000+08:00|                                                   5.0| |2020-01-01T00:00:07.000+08:00|                                                   7.0| |2020-01-01T00:00:09.000+08:00|                                                   9.0| +-----------------------------+------------------------------------------------------+
```

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--_3-17-segment"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--3.17-segment"></a>

### [3.17 Segment](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--_3-17-segment)

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-20-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-21"></a>

#### [Registration statement](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-20)

```sql
create function segment as 'org.apache.iotdb.library.dprofile.UDTFSegment'
```

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-20-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-21"></a>

#### [Usage](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-20)

This function is used to segment a time series into subsequences according to linear trend, and returns linear fitted values of first values in each subsequence or every data point.

**Name:** SEGMENT

**Input Series:** Only support a single input series. The type is INT32 / INT64 / FLOAT / DOUBLE.

**Parameters:**

- `output` :"all" to output all fitted points; "first" to output first fitted points in each subsequence.
- `error`: error allowed at linear regression. It is defined as mean absolute error of a subsequence.

**Output Series:** Output a single series. The type is DOUBLE.

**Note:** This function treat input series as equal-interval sampled. All data are loaded, so downsample input series first if there are too many data points.

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-20-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-21"></a>

#### [Examples](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-20)

Input series:

```
+-----------------------------+------------+
|                         Time|root.test.s1|
+-----------------------------+------------+
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.000+08:00|         5.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.100+08:00|         0.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.200+08:00|         1.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.300+08:00|         2.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.400+08:00|         3.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.500+08:00|         4.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.600+08:00|         5.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.700+08:00|         6.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.800+08:00|         7.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.900+08:00|         8.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.000+08:00|         9.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.100+08:00|         9.1|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.200+08:00|         9.2|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.300+08:00|         9.3|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.400+08:00|         9.4|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.500+08:00|         9.5|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.600+08:00|         9.6|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.700+08:00|         9.7|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.800+08:00|         9.8|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.900+08:00|         9.9|
|1970-01-01T08:00:02.000+08:00|        10.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:02.100+08:00|         8.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:02.200+08:00|         6.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:02.300+08:00|         4.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:02.400+08:00|         2.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:02.500+08:00|         0.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:02.600+08:00|        -2.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:02.700+08:00|        -4.0|

|1970-01-01T08:00:02.900+08:00|        -8.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:03.000+08:00|       -10.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:03.100+08:00|        10.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:03.200+08:00|        10.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:03.300+08:00|        10.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:03.400+08:00|        10.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:03.500+08:00|        10.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:03.600+08:00|        10.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:03.700+08:00|        10.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:03.800+08:00|        10.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:03.900+08:00|        10.0|
+-----------------------------+------------+
```

SQL for query:

```sql
select segment(s1, "error"="0.1") from root.test
```

Output series:

```
+-----------------------------+------------------------------------+ |                         Time|segment(root.test.s1, "error"="0.1")| +-----------------------------+------------------------------------+ |1970-01-01T08:00:00.000+08:00|                                 5.0| |1970-01-01T08:00:00.200+08:00|                                 1.0| |1970-01-01T08:00:01.000+08:00|                                 9.0| |1970-01-01T08:00:02.000+08:00|                                10.0| |1970-01-01T08:00:03.000+08:00|                               -10.0| |1970-01-01T08:00:03.200+08:00|                                10.0| +-----------------------------+------------------------------------+
```

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--_3-18-skew"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--3.18-skew"></a>

### [3.18 Skew](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--_3-18-skew)

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-21-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-22"></a>

#### [Registration statement](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-21)

```sql
create function skew as 'org.apache.iotdb.library.dprofile.UDAFSkew'
```

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-21-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-22"></a>

#### [Usage](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-21)

This function is used to calculate the population skewness.

**Name:** SKEW

**Input Series:** Only support a single input series. The type is INT32 / INT64 / FLOAT / DOUBLE.

**Output Series:** Output a single series. The type is DOUBLE. There is only one data point in the series, whose timestamp is 0 and value is the population skewness.

**Note:** Missing points, null points and `NaN` in the input series will be ignored.

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-21-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-22"></a>

#### [Examples](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-21)

Input series:

```
+-----------------------------+---------------+
|                         Time|root.test.d1.s1|
+-----------------------------+---------------+
|2020-01-01T00:00:00.000+08:00|            1.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:01.000+08:00|            2.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:02.000+08:00|            3.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:03.000+08:00|            4.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:04.000+08:00|            5.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:05.000+08:00|            6.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:06.000+08:00|            7.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:07.000+08:00|            8.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:08.000+08:00|            9.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:09.000+08:00|           10.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:10.000+08:00|           10.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:11.000+08:00|           10.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:12.000+08:00|           10.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:13.000+08:00|           10.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:14.000+08:00|           10.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:15.000+08:00|           10.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:16.000+08:00|           10.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:17.000+08:00|           10.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:18.000+08:00|           10.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:19.000+08:00|           10.0|
+-----------------------------+---------------+
```

SQL for query:

```sql
select skew(s1) from root.test.d1
```

Output series:

```
+-----------------------------+-----------------------+
|                         Time|  skew(root.test.d1.s1)|
+-----------------------------+-----------------------+
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.000+08:00|    -0.9998427402292644|
+-----------------------------+-----------------------+
```

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--_3-19-spline"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--3.19-spline"></a>

### [3.19 Spline](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--_3-19-spline)

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-22-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-23"></a>

#### [Registration statement](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-22)

```sql
create function spline as 'org.apache.iotdb.library.dprofile.UDTFSpline'
```

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-22-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-23"></a>

#### [Usage](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-22)

This function is used to calculate cubic spline interpolation of input series.

**Name:** SPLINE

**Input Series:** Only support a single input series. The type is INT32 / INT64 / FLOAT / DOUBLE.

- `points`: Number of resampling points.

**Output Series:** Output a single series. The type is DOUBLE.

> [!NOTE]
> : Output series retains the first and last timestamps of input series. Interpolation points are selected at equal intervals. The function tries to calculate only when there are no less than 4 points in input series.

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-22-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-23"></a>

#### [Examples](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-22)

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--assigning-number-of-interpolation-points"></a>

##### [Assigning number of interpolation points](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--assigning-number-of-interpolation-points)

Input series:

```
+-----------------------------+------------+ |                         Time|root.test.s1| +-----------------------------+------------+ |1970-01-01T08:00:00.000+08:00|         0.0| |1970-01-01T08:00:00.300+08:00|         1.2| |1970-01-01T08:00:00.500+08:00|         1.7| |1970-01-01T08:00:00.700+08:00|         2.0| |1970-01-01T08:00:00.900+08:00|         2.1| |1970-01-01T08:00:01.100+08:00|         2.0| |1970-01-01T08:00:01.200+08:00|         1.8| |1970-01-01T08:00:01.300+08:00|         1.2| |1970-01-01T08:00:01.400+08:00|         1.0| |1970-01-01T08:00:01.500+08:00|         1.6| +-----------------------------+------------+
```

SQL for query:

```sql
select spline(s1, "points"="151") from root.test
```

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--_3-20-spread"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--3.20-spread"></a>

### [3.20 Spread](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--_3-20-spread)

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-23-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-24"></a>

#### [Registration statement](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-23)

```sql
create function spread as 'org.apache.iotdb.library.dprofile.UDAFSpread'
```

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-23-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-24"></a>

#### [Usage](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-23)

This function is used to calculate the spread of time series, that is, the maximum value minus the minimum value.

**Name:** SPREAD

**Input Series:** Only support a single input series. The type is INT32 / INT64 / FLOAT / DOUBLE.

**Output Series:** Output a single series. The type is the same as the input. There is only one data point in the series, whose timestamp is 0 and value is the spread.

**Note:** Missing points, null points and `NaN` in the input series will be ignored.

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-23-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-24"></a>

#### [Examples](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-23)

Input series:

```
+-----------------------------+---------------+
|                         Time|root.test.d1.s1|
+-----------------------------+---------------+
|2020-01-01T00:00:02.000+08:00|          100.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:03.000+08:00|          101.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:04.000+08:00|          102.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:06.000+08:00|          104.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:08.000+08:00|          126.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:10.000+08:00|          108.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:14.000+08:00|          112.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:15.000+08:00|          113.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:16.000+08:00|          114.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:18.000+08:00|          116.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:20.000+08:00|          118.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:22.000+08:00|          120.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:26.000+08:00|          124.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:28.000+08:00|          126.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:30.000+08:00|            NaN|
+-----------------------------+---------------+
```

SQL for query:

```sql
select spread(s1) from root.test.d1 where time <= 2020-01-01 00:00:30
```

Output series:

```
+-----------------------------+-----------------------+
|                         Time|spread(root.test.d1.s1)|
+-----------------------------+-----------------------+
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.000+08:00|                   26.0|
+-----------------------------+-----------------------+
```

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--_3-21-zscore"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--3.21-zscore"></a>

### [3.21 ZScore](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--_3-21-zscore)

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-24-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-25"></a>

#### [Registration statement](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-24)

```sql
create function zscore as 'org.apache.iotdb.library.dprofile.UDTFZScore'
```

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-24-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-25"></a>

#### [Usage](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-24)

This function is used to standardize the input series with z-score.

**Name:** ZSCORE

**Input Series:** Only support a single input series. The type is INT32 / INT64 / FLOAT / DOUBLE.

- `compute`: When set to "batch", anomaly test is conducted after importing all data points; when set to "stream", it is required to provide mean and standard deviation. The default method is "batch".
- `avg`: Mean value when method is set to "stream".
- `sd`: Standard deviation when method is set to "stream".

**Output Series:** Output a single series. The type is DOUBLE.

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-24-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-25"></a>

#### [Examples](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-24)

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--batch-computing-2-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--batch-computing-3"></a>

##### [Batch computing](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--batch-computing-2)

Input series:

```
+-----------------------------+------------+
|                         Time|root.test.s1|
+-----------------------------+------------+
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.100+08:00|         0.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.200+08:00|         0.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.300+08:00|         1.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.400+08:00|        -1.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.500+08:00|         0.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.600+08:00|         0.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.700+08:00|        -2.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.800+08:00|         2.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.900+08:00|         0.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.000+08:00|         0.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.100+08:00|         1.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.200+08:00|        -1.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.300+08:00|        -1.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.400+08:00|         1.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.500+08:00|         0.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.600+08:00|         0.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.700+08:00|        10.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.800+08:00|         2.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.900+08:00|        -2.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:02.000+08:00|         0.0|
+-----------------------------+------------+
```

SQL for query:

```sql
select zscore(s1) from root.test
```

Output series:

```
+-----------------------------+--------------------+
|                         Time|zscore(root.test.s1)|
+-----------------------------+--------------------+
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.100+08:00|-0.20672455764868078|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.200+08:00|-0.20672455764868078|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.300+08:00| 0.20672455764868078|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.400+08:00| -0.6201736729460423|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.500+08:00|-0.20672455764868078|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.600+08:00|-0.20672455764868078|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.700+08:00|  -1.033622788243404|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.800+08:00|  0.6201736729460423|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.900+08:00|-0.20672455764868078|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.000+08:00|-0.20672455764868078|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.100+08:00| 0.20672455764868078|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.200+08:00| -0.6201736729460423|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.300+08:00| -0.6201736729460423|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.400+08:00| 0.20672455764868078|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.500+08:00|-0.20672455764868078|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.600+08:00|-0.20672455764868078|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.700+08:00|  3.9277665953249348|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.800+08:00|  0.6201736729460423|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.900+08:00|  -1.033622788243404|
|1970-01-01T08:00:02.000+08:00|-0.20672455764868078|
```

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--_4-anomaly-detection"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--4.-anomaly-detection"></a>

## [4. Anomaly Detection](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--_4-anomaly-detection)

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--_4-1-iqr"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--4.1-iqr"></a>

### [4.1 IQR](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--_4-1-iqr)

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-25-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-26"></a>

#### [Registration statement](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-25)

```sql
create function iqr as 'org.apache.iotdb.library.anomaly.UDTFIQR'
```

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-25-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-26"></a>

#### [Usage](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-25)

This function is used to detect anomalies based on IQR. Points distributing beyond 1.5 times IQR are selected.

**Name:** IQR

**Input Series:** Only support a single input series. The type is INT32 / INT64 / FLOAT / DOUBLE.

- `method`: When set to "batch", anomaly test is conducted after importing all data points; when set to "stream", it is required to provide upper and lower quantiles. The default method is "batch".
- `q1`: The lower quantile when method is set to "stream".
- `q3`: The upper quantile when method is set to "stream".

**Output Series:** Output a single series. The type is DOUBLE.

**Note:**

<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><semantics><mrow><mi>I</mi><mi>Q</mi><mi>R</mi><mo>=</mo><msub><mi>Q</mi><mn>3</mn></msub><mo>−</mo><msub><mi>Q</mi><mn>1</mn></msub></mrow><annotation>IQR=Q_3-Q_1</annotation></semantics></math>

IQR=Q3−Q1

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-25-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-26"></a>

#### [Examples](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-25)

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--batch-computing-3-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--batch-computing-4"></a>

##### [Batch computing](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--batch-computing-3)

Input series:

```
+-----------------------------+------------+
|                         Time|root.test.s1|
+-----------------------------+------------+
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.100+08:00|         0.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.200+08:00|         0.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.300+08:00|         1.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.400+08:00|        -1.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.500+08:00|         0.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.600+08:00|         0.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.700+08:00|        -2.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.800+08:00|         2.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.900+08:00|         0.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.000+08:00|         0.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.100+08:00|         1.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.200+08:00|        -1.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.300+08:00|        -1.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.400+08:00|         1.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.500+08:00|         0.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.600+08:00|         0.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.700+08:00|        10.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.800+08:00|         2.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.900+08:00|        -2.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:02.000+08:00|         0.0|
+-----------------------------+------------+
```

SQL for query:

```sql
select iqr(s1) from root.test
```

Output series:

```
+-----------------------------+-----------------+
|                         Time|iqr(root.test.s1)|
+-----------------------------+-----------------+
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.700+08:00|             10.0|
+-----------------------------+-----------------+
```

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--_4-2-ksigma"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--4.2-ksigma"></a>

### [4.2 KSigma](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--_4-2-ksigma)

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-26-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-27"></a>

#### [Registration statement](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-26)

```sql
create function ksigma as 'org.apache.iotdb.library.anomaly.UDTFKSigma'
```

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-26-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-27"></a>

#### [Usage](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-26)

This function is used to detect anomalies based on the Dynamic K-Sigma Algorithm. Within a sliding window, the input value with a deviation of more than k times the standard deviation from the average will be output as anomaly.

**Name:** KSIGMA

**Input Series:** Only support a single input series. The type is INT32 / INT64 / FLOAT / DOUBLE.

- `k`: How many times to multiply on standard deviation to define anomaly, the default value is 3.
- `window`: The window size of Dynamic K-Sigma Algorithm, the default value is 10000.

**Output Series:** Output a single series. The type is same as input series.

**Note:** Only when is larger than 0, the anomaly detection will be performed. Otherwise, nothing will be output.

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-26-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-27"></a>

#### [Examples](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-26)

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--assigning-k"></a>

##### [Assigning k](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--assigning-k)

Input series:

```
+-----------------------------+---------------+
|                         Time|root.test.d1.s1|
+-----------------------------+---------------+
|2020-01-01T00:00:02.000+08:00|            0.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:03.000+08:00|           50.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:04.000+08:00|          100.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:06.000+08:00|          150.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:08.000+08:00|          200.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:10.000+08:00|          200.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:14.000+08:00|          200.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:15.000+08:00|          200.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:16.000+08:00|          200.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:18.000+08:00|          200.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:20.000+08:00|          150.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:22.000+08:00|          100.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:26.000+08:00|           50.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:28.000+08:00|            0.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:30.000+08:00|            NaN|
+-----------------------------+---------------+
```

SQL for query:

```sql
select ksigma(s1,"k"="1.0") from root.test.d1 where time <= 2020-01-01 00:00:30
```

Output series:

```
+-----------------------------+---------------------------------+ |Time                         |ksigma(root.test.d1.s1,"k"="3.0")| +-----------------------------+---------------------------------+ |2020-01-01T00:00:02.000+08:00|                              0.0| |2020-01-01T00:00:03.000+08:00|                             50.0| |2020-01-01T00:00:26.000+08:00|                             50.0| |2020-01-01T00:00:28.000+08:00|                              0.0| +-----------------------------+---------------------------------+
```

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--_4-3-lof"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--4.3-lof"></a>

### [4.3 LOF](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--_4-3-lof)

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-27-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-28"></a>

#### [Registration statement](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-27)

```sql
create function LOF as 'org.apache.iotdb.library.anomaly.UDTFLOF'
```

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-27-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-28"></a>

#### [Usage](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-27)

This function is used to detect density anomaly of time series. According to k-th distance calculation parameter and local outlier factor (lof) threshold, the function judges if a set of input values is an density anomaly, and a bool mark of anomaly values will be output.

**Name:** LOF

**Input Series:** Multiple input series. The type is INT32 / INT64 / FLOAT / DOUBLE.

- `method`:assign a detection method. The default value is "default", when input data has multiple dimensions. The alternative is "series", when a input series will be transformed to high dimension.
- `k`:use the k-th distance to calculate lof. Default value is 3.
- `window`: size of window to split origin data points. Default value is 10000.
- `windowsize`:dimension that will be transformed into when method is "series". The default value is 5.

**Output Series:** Output a single series. The type is DOUBLE.

**Note:** Incomplete rows will be ignored. They are neither calculated nor marked as anomaly.

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-27-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-28"></a>

#### [Examples](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-27)

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--using-default-parameters"></a>

##### [Using default parameters](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--using-default-parameters)

Input series:

```
+-----------------------------+---------------+---------------+ |                         Time|root.test.d1.s1|root.test.d1.s2| +-----------------------------+---------------+---------------+ |1970-01-01T08:00:00.100+08:00|            0.0|            0.0| |1970-01-01T08:00:00.200+08:00|            0.0|            1.0| |1970-01-01T08:00:00.300+08:00|            1.0|            1.0| |1970-01-01T08:00:00.400+08:00|            1.0|            0.0| |1970-01-01T08:00:00.500+08:00|            0.0|           -1.0| |1970-01-01T08:00:00.600+08:00|           -1.0|           -1.0| |1970-01-01T08:00:00.700+08:00|           -1.0|            0.0| |1970-01-01T08:00:00.800+08:00|            2.0|            2.0| |1970-01-01T08:00:00.900+08:00|            0.0|           null| +-----------------------------+---------------+---------------+
```

SQL for query:

```sql
select lof(s1,s2) from root.test.d1 where time<1000
```

Output series:

```
+-----------------------------+-------------------------------------+ |                         Time|lof(root.test.d1.s1, root.test.d1.s2)| +-----------------------------+-------------------------------------+ |1970-01-01T08:00:00.100+08:00|                   3.8274824267668244| |1970-01-01T08:00:00.200+08:00|                   3.0117631741126156| |1970-01-01T08:00:00.300+08:00|                    2.838155437762879| |1970-01-01T08:00:00.400+08:00|                   3.0117631741126156| |1970-01-01T08:00:00.500+08:00|                     2.73518261244453| |1970-01-01T08:00:00.600+08:00|                    2.371440975708148| |1970-01-01T08:00:00.700+08:00|                     2.73518261244453| |1970-01-01T08:00:00.800+08:00|                   1.7561416374270742| +-----------------------------+-------------------------------------+
```

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--diagnosing-1d-timeseries"></a>

##### [Diagnosing 1d timeseries](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--diagnosing-1d-timeseries)

Input series:

```
+-----------------------------+---------------+
|                         Time|root.test.d1.s1|
+-----------------------------+---------------+
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.100+08:00|            1.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.200+08:00|            2.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.300+08:00|            3.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.400+08:00|            4.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.500+08:00|            5.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.600+08:00|            6.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.700+08:00|            7.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.800+08:00|            8.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.900+08:00|            9.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.000+08:00|           10.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.100+08:00|           11.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.200+08:00|           12.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.300+08:00|           13.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.400+08:00|           14.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.500+08:00|           15.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.600+08:00|           16.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.700+08:00|           17.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.800+08:00|           18.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.900+08:00|           19.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:02.000+08:00|           20.0|
+-----------------------------+---------------+
```

SQL for query:

```sql
select lof(s1, "method"="series") from root.test.d1 where time<1000
```

Output series:

```
+-----------------------------+--------------------+
|                         Time|lof(root.test.d1.s1)|
+-----------------------------+--------------------+
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.100+08:00|    3.77777777777778|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.200+08:00|    4.32727272727273|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.300+08:00|    4.85714285714286|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.400+08:00|    5.40909090909091|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.500+08:00|    5.94999999999999|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.600+08:00|    6.43243243243243|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.700+08:00|    6.79999999999999|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.800+08:00|                 7.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.900+08:00|                 7.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.000+08:00|    6.79999999999999|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.100+08:00|    6.43243243243243|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.200+08:00|    5.94999999999999|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.300+08:00|    5.40909090909091|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.400+08:00|    4.85714285714286|

|1970-01-01T08:00:01.600+08:00|    3.77777777777778|

```

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--_4-4-missdetect"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--4.4-missdetect"></a>

### [4.4 MissDetect](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--_4-4-missdetect)

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-28-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-29"></a>

#### [Registration statement](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-28)

```sql
create function missdetect as 'org.apache.iotdb.library.anomaly.UDTFMissDetect'
```

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-28-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-29"></a>

#### [Usage](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-28)

This function is used to detect missing anomalies. In some datasets, missing values are filled by linear interpolation. Thus, there are several long perfect linear segments. By discovering these perfect linear segments, missing anomalies are detected.

**Name:** MISSDETECT

**Input Series:** Only support a single input series. The data type is INT32 / INT64 / FLOAT / DOUBLE.

**Parameter:**

`error`: The minimum length of the detected missing anomalies, which is an integer greater than or equal to 10. By default, it is 10.

**Output Series:** Output a single series. The type is BOOLEAN. Each data point which is miss anomaly will be labeled as true.

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-28-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-29"></a>

#### [Examples](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-28)

Input series:

```
+-----------------------------+---------------+
|                         Time|root.test.d2.s2|
+-----------------------------+---------------+
|2021-07-01T12:00:00.000+08:00|            0.0|
|2021-07-01T12:00:01.000+08:00|            1.0|
|2021-07-01T12:00:02.000+08:00|            0.0|
|2021-07-01T12:00:03.000+08:00|            1.0|
|2021-07-01T12:00:04.000+08:00|            0.0|
|2021-07-01T12:00:05.000+08:00|            0.0|
|2021-07-01T12:00:06.000+08:00|            0.0|
|2021-07-01T12:00:07.000+08:00|            0.0|
|2021-07-01T12:00:08.000+08:00|            0.0|
|2021-07-01T12:00:09.000+08:00|            0.0|
|2021-07-01T12:00:10.000+08:00|            0.0|
|2021-07-01T12:00:11.000+08:00|            0.0|
|2021-07-01T12:00:12.000+08:00|            0.0|
|2021-07-01T12:00:13.000+08:00|            0.0|
|2021-07-01T12:00:14.000+08:00|            0.0|
|2021-07-01T12:00:15.000+08:00|            0.0|
|2021-07-01T12:00:16.000+08:00|            1.0|
|2021-07-01T12:00:17.000+08:00|            0.0|
|2021-07-01T12:00:18.000+08:00|            1.0|
|2021-07-01T12:00:19.000+08:00|            0.0|
|2021-07-01T12:00:20.000+08:00|            1.0|
+-----------------------------+---------------+
```

SQL for query:

```sql
select missdetect(s2,'minlen'='10') from root.test.d2
```

Output series:

```
+-----------------------------+------------------------------------------+
|                         Time|missdetect(root.test.d2.s2, "minlen"="10")|
+-----------------------------+------------------------------------------+
|2021-07-01T12:00:00.000+08:00|                                     false|
|2021-07-01T12:00:01.000+08:00|                                     false|
|2021-07-01T12:00:02.000+08:00|                                     false|
|2021-07-01T12:00:03.000+08:00|                                     false|
|2021-07-01T12:00:04.000+08:00|                                      true|
|2021-07-01T12:00:05.000+08:00|                                      true|
|2021-07-01T12:00:06.000+08:00|                                      true|
|2021-07-01T12:00:07.000+08:00|                                      true|
|2021-07-01T12:00:08.000+08:00|                                      true|
|2021-07-01T12:00:09.000+08:00|                                      true|
|2021-07-01T12:00:10.000+08:00|                                      true|
|2021-07-01T12:00:11.000+08:00|                                      true|
|2021-07-01T12:00:12.000+08:00|                                      true|
|2021-07-01T12:00:13.000+08:00|                                      true|
|2021-07-01T12:00:14.000+08:00|                                      true|
|2021-07-01T12:00:15.000+08:00|                                      true|
|2021-07-01T12:00:16.000+08:00|                                     false|
|2021-07-01T12:00:17.000+08:00|                                     false|
|2021-07-01T12:00:18.000+08:00|                                     false|
|2021-07-01T12:00:19.000+08:00|                                     false|
|2021-07-01T12:00:20.000+08:00|                                     false|

```

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--_4-5-range"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--4.5-range"></a>

### [4.5 Range](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--_4-5-range)

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-29-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-30"></a>

#### [Registration statement](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-29)

```sql
create function range as 'org.apache.iotdb.library.anomaly.UDTFRange'
```

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-29-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-30"></a>

#### [Usage](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-29)

This function is used to detect range anomaly of time series. According to upper bound and lower bound parameters, the function judges if a input value is beyond range, aka range anomaly, and a new time series of anomaly will be output.

**Name:** RANGE

**Input Series:** Only support a single input series. The type is INT32 / INT64 / FLOAT / DOUBLE.

- `lower_bound`:lower bound of range anomaly detection.
- `upper_bound`:upper bound of range anomaly detection.

**Output Series:** Output a single series. The type is the same as the input.

**Note:** Only when `upper_bound` is larger than `lower_bound`, the anomaly detection will be performed. Otherwise, nothing will be output.

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-29-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-30"></a>

#### [Examples](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-29)

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--assigning-lower-and-upper-bound"></a>

##### [Assigning Lower and Upper Bound](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--assigning-lower-and-upper-bound)

Input series:

```
+-----------------------------+---------------+
|                         Time|root.test.d1.s1|
+-----------------------------+---------------+
|2020-01-01T00:00:02.000+08:00|          100.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:03.000+08:00|          101.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:04.000+08:00|          102.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:06.000+08:00|          104.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:08.000+08:00|          126.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:10.000+08:00|          108.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:14.000+08:00|          112.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:15.000+08:00|          113.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:16.000+08:00|          114.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:18.000+08:00|          116.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:20.000+08:00|          118.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:22.000+08:00|          120.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:26.000+08:00|          124.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:28.000+08:00|          126.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:30.000+08:00|            NaN|
+-----------------------------+---------------+
```

SQL for query:

```sql
select range(s1,"lower_bound"="101.0","upper_bound"="125.0") from root.test.d1 where time <= 2020-01-01 00:00:30
```

Output series:

```
+-----------------------------+------------------------------------------------------------------+
|Time                         |range(root.test.d1.s1,"lower_bound"="101.0","upper_bound"="125.0")|
+-----------------------------+------------------------------------------------------------------+
|2020-01-01T00:00:02.000+08:00|                                                             100.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:08.000+08:00|                                                             126.0|
|2020-01-01T00:00:28.000+08:00|                                                             126.0|
+-----------------------------+------------------------------------------------------------------+
```

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--_4-6-twosidedfilter"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--4.6-twosidedfilter"></a>

### [4.6 TwoSidedFilter](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--_4-6-twosidedfilter)

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-30-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-31"></a>

#### [Registration statement](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--registration-statement-30)

```sql
create function twosidedfilter as 'org.apache.iotdb.library.anomaly.UDTFTwoSidedFilter'
```

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-30-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-31"></a>

#### [Usage](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--usage-30)

The function is used to filter anomalies of a numeric time series based on two-sided window detection.

**Name:** TWOSIDEDFILTER

**Input Series:** Only support a single input series. The data type is INT32 / INT64 / FLOAT / DOUBLE

**Output Series:** Output a single series. The type is the same as the input. It is the input without anomalies.

**Parameter:**

- `len`: The size of the window, which is a positive integer. By default, it's 5. When `len`=3, the algorithm detects forward window and backward window with length 3 and calculates the outlierness of the current point.
- `threshold`: The threshold of outlierness, which is a floating number in (0,1). By default, it's 0.3. The strict standard of detecting anomalies is in proportion to the threshold.

<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-30-2"></a>
<a id="sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-31"></a>

#### [Examples](#sql-manual-udf-libraries_apache--examples-30)

Input series:

```
+-----------------------------+------------+
|                         Time|root.test.s0|
+-----------------------------+------------+
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.000+08:00|      2002.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.000+08:00|      1946.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:02.000+08:00|      1958.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:03.000+08:00|      2012.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:04.000+08:00|      2051.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:05.000+08:00|      1898.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:06.000+08:00|      2014.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:07.000+08:00|      2052.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:08.000+08:00|      1935.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:09.000+08:00|      1901.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:10.000+08:00|      1972.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:11.000+08:00|      1969.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:12.000+08:00|      1984.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:13.000+08:00|      2018.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:37.000+08:00|      1484.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:38.000+08:00|      1055.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:39.000+08:00|      1050.0|
|1970-01-01T08:01:05.000+08:00|      1023.0|
|1970-01-01T08:01:06.000+08:00|      1056.0|
|1970-01-01T08:01:07.000+08:00|       978.0|
|1970-01-01T08:01:08.000+08:00|      1050.0|
|1970-01-01T08:01:09.000+08:00|      1123.0|
|1970-01-01T08:01:10.000+08:00|      1150.0|
|1970-01-01T08:01:11.000+08:00|      1034.0|
|1970-01-01T08:01:12.000+08:00|       950.0|
|1970-01-01T08:01:13.000+08:00|      1059.0|
+-----------------------------+------------+
```

SQL for query:

```sql
select TwoSidedFilter(s0, 'len'='5', 'threshold'='0.3') from root.test
```

Output series:

```
+-----------------------------+------------+
|                         Time|root.test.s0|
+-----------------------------+------------+
|1970-01-01T08:00:00.000+08:00|      2002.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:01.000+08:00|      1946.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:02.000+08:00|      1958.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:03.000+08:00|      2012.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:04.000+08:00|      2051.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:05.000+08:00|      1898.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:06.000+08:00|      2014.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:07.000+08:00|      2052.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:08.000+08:00|      1935.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:09.000+08:00|      1901.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:10.000+08:00|      1972.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:11.000+08:00|      1969.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:12.000+08:00|      1984.0|
|1970-01-01T08:00:13.000+08:00|      2018.0|
|1970-01-01T08:01:05.000+08:00|      1023.0|
|1970-01-01T08:01:06.000+08:00|      1056.0|
|1970-01-01T08:01:07.000+08:00|       978.0|
|1970-01-01T08:01:08.000+08:00|      1050.0|
|1970-01-01T08:01:09.000+08:00|      1123.0|
|1970-01-01T08:01:10.000+08:00|      1150.0|
|1970-01-01T08:01:11.000+08:00|      1034.0|
```
